---
title: "Translating iptables to firewall-cmd"
date: 2018-07-29
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless
---

### Post by rsteinmetz70112 on 2018-07-29
I am moving from 16.04 to 18.04. I want to adopt firewalld but am having difficulty understanding firewall-cmd commands. Here is a part of my iptables.rules file from 16.04. What would be the correct commands for firewall-cmd or is there a better way to accomplish this. I only need to forward the ipv4 addresses.

```
# Generated by iptables-save v1.6.0 
*nat
:PREROUTING ACCEPT [66316:6289839]
:INPUT ACCEPT [33999:3265034]
:OUTPUT ACCEPT [10562:903361]
:POSTROUTING ACCEPT [4:240]
-A PREROUTING -d 111.222.333.444/32 -j DNAT --to-destination 192.168.1.1
-A POSTROUTING -s 192.168.1.1/32 -j SNAT --to-source 111.222.333.444
-A POSTROUTING -o enp2s0 -j MASQUERADE
```

---

### Post by rsteinmetz70112 on 2018-08-04
I found the answer

```
firewall-cmd --permanent --direct --passthrough { ipv4 | ipv6 | eb } args
```
args = any iptables command 
The command below succeeds. Now for more testing
```
firewall-cmd --permanent --direct --passthrough -A PREROUTING -d 111.222.333.444/32 -j DNAT --to-destination 192.168.1.1
```

---
